and what I want to say, I've got three points.
The first I want to make is speciesism,
which is in a talk by Jane Goodall.
I discovered how humans distance themselves from animals on
purpose to justify their exploitation.
We are actually all connected.
The organs of pigs are so similar to humans
that they're used for human cures as in diabetes
and as as in heart value replacement.
The connection isn't just physical.
Jane Goodall heavily emphasized our emotional connections.
Ricky, the same he has said, animals are not here for us
to do as we pleased with.
We are not their superiors. We are their equals.
We are their family. Be kind to them.
The second point I want to make is respect for the law.
And a couple of your speakers have already said this
by changing the time taking for farmers
to substitute farrow and crates.
I feel you've disrespected all the lawyers,
all the grassroots workers,
the justice system, and the pigs.
You've undermined public trust.
I'm appalled and insulted by this disregard,
and I feel a lot of anger also at the fact that over
and over again, we have to say that powering crates,
caging any animal is cruel and it's wrong.
The third point I want to make is economics versus ethics.
I've noticed that this particular government,
maybe it's just media, I don't know,
but it seems to me very much that all the rules
that have been reversed and has been locked, um, are
to favor economics, rather over favoring humanity.
I haven't studied economics, but I know how to budget
and I know how to triage
and I know what's more important to ease suffering
or the goss national GDP.
Our world needs less materialism
and more care for many reasons.
Ecological, psychological, and ethical with animals
and cages, you're virtually paying for their torture.
And it is torture that's not too strong a language.
It's mental and physical torture.
And I would really, really like you to just for one minute,
see how you would feel stuck in a bowel crate.
Even just for one week.
I think you'd be yearning for the physio to come
and ease your aching joints.
I used to be a nurse, and that's what we did in hospital.
Someone had a stroke, their joints can't move.
Joints are made to move.
So by we, by passive movement, we used to move their joints
or, um, relieve them with massage.
Brains or minds also need interesting stimulation.
These things are inherent instincts,
which the law said we should be observing in animals.
I hope that you can feel the pig's point of view.
I really hope you can. And I hope
that you'll please stop the faring crates as from next year
as was agreed, the agreement made in 2020.
And that's all I have to say.
Thank you Lith. Uh, what Happens now?
Oh, So we now have a question from Steve Abel.
Yep. Thank, thank you very much, lith.
Uh, that's a very clear submission.
Um, I wonder if you can expand on
what you alluded to, is that, um,
it's actually in greater Good. Can you hear me okay?
I can't hear you. Ah, can you hear me?
Oh, we're having a few issues here.
Can you hear me? That's all right.
